note: Pilot cleanup projects (Philippines). Priority = Severity × Exposure × Ecology (each 1–5) → 0–100. The donation card (Option 1c 'Tangible Ask') shows an impact calculator: amount chips → kg removed (amount/50 × impact_per50) → a bar maxing at impact_ceiling_kg. impact_per50 derives from local $0.50–$2.50/kg cleanup ($50 ≈ 20–100 kg). All figures are modeled estimates, NOT guarantees; the picked amount is a PREVIEW — the actual gift is entered on the org's own page.

projects.name: Manila Bay & the Pasig River
projects.problem: One of the world's highest-emitting plastic rivers — ~41,000 tonnes a year — flowing into a living bay.
projects.readout_sub: of plastic pulled from the Pasig & Manila Bay

projects.name: Malapascua & northern Cebu
projects.problem: Reefs that draw divers from across the world, fouled by plastic riding in on the tide and from the towns.
projects.readout_sub: of plastic pulled from Malapascua's reefs & beaches

projects.name: Siargao Island
projects.problem: A surf-and-mangrove island where the plastic keeps washing back onto the beaches it depends on.
projects.readout_sub: of plastic pulled from Siargao's coasts & mangroves

projects.name: Negros Oriental coast (Zamboanguita)
projects.problem: Coral and seagrass coasts where a local crew pulls plastic from the water nearly every day.
projects.readout_sub: of plastic pulled from the Zamboanguita reef coast

projects.name: Stop it upstream
projects.problem: Cleanup is downstream. The cheapest tonne to remove is the one that never reaches the sea — so this funds prevention, not pickup.
